channel.title: NYT > Technology
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Elon Musk’s Lawyers Ask OpenAI’s President Why He Is Worth $30 Billion
item.description: The legal team implied in a federal trial that Greg Brockman, OpenAI’s president and co-founder, was driven by greed rather than building safe A.I.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz
item.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
item.media:description: OpenAI’s Greg Brockman entering federal court on Monday with his wife, Anna Brockman.

item.title: White House Considers Vetting A.I. Models Before They Are Released
item.description: The Trump administration, which took a noninterventionist approach to artificial intelligence, is now discussing imposing oversight on A.I. models before they are made publicly available.
item.dc:creator: Tripp Mickle, Julian E. Barnes, Sheera Frenkel and Dustin Volz
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Trump administration’s shift in policy began after Anthropic introduced a powerful artificial intelligence model called Mythos.

item.title: S.E.C. Settles Lawsuit Against Elon Musk Over His Twitter Disclosures
item.description: The agency, which has been pulling back on lawsuits against major companies, ended a case that had accused Mr. Musk of hiding his purchases of Twitter stock. He agreed to pay $1.5 million.
item.dc:creator: Cecilia Kang
item.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
item.media:description: Elon Musk arriving at federal court in San Francisco in March.

item.title: Anthropic and Wall Street Giants Join Forces to Create New A.I. Firm
item.description: Blackstone and Goldman Sachs are among the investors in the new firm, which will help integrate Anthropic’s A.I. model Claude into their systems.
item.dc:creator: Maureen Farrell
item.media:credit: Marissa Leshnov for The New York Times
item.media:description: An Anthropic office in San Francisco.

item.title: Anthropic’s C.E.O. Says It Could Grow by 80 Times This Year
item.description: The chief executive, Dario Amodei, said the rapid growth had exponentially increased the start-up’s need for more computing power.
item.dc:creator: Natallie Rocha
item.media:credit: Denis Balibouse/Reuters
item.media:description: Anthropic’s chief executive, Dario Amodei, in January. On Wednesday, he spoke at his company’s annual developer conference.

item.title: Elon Musk’s Confidante Shivon Zilis Is Cast as His Inside Source at OpenAI
item.description: Shivon Zilis worked closely with Elon Musk while she was on OpenAI’s board. Her ties to the world’s richest man were detailed in a landmark trial on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz and Mike Isaac
item.media:credit: Brennan Smart for The New York Times
item.media:description: Shivon Zilis, a longtime confidante of Elon Musk and the mother of four of his children, testified in a federal trial on Wednesday.

item.title: Meditating or Rebooting? A Robot Buddhist Monk Comes to Korea.
item.description: Gabi, the newest monk at a temple in Seoul, vowed to not overcharge as it seeks enlightenment.
item.dc:creator: Remy Tumin
item.media:credit: Yonhap News Agency, via via Reuters

item.title: American Factories Lag in Adopting A.I. This Drugmaker Is an Exception.
item.description: A Bristol Myers Squibb plant that makes cancer drugs was the only manufacturer in the U.S. recognized for innovation by the World Economic Forum this year.
item.dc:creator: Farah Stockman
item.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
item.media:description: At the Bristol Myers Squibb facility in Devens, Mass., scientists use automated machines to help make cancer drugs.

item.title: Elon Musk Wanted OpenAI to Go Commercial, Greg Brockman Testifies
item.description: Greg Brockman, OpenAI’s president, testified in a trial pitting Mr. Musk against his company that the world’s richest man was eager to change how it operated as a nonprofit.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz and Mike Isaac
item.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
item.media:description: OpenAI’s president, Greg Brockman, with his wife, Anna Brockman, at the federal courthouse in Oakland, Calif., on Tuesday. He testified for a second day.

item.title: Apple Reaches $250 Million Settlement Over Claims It Misled People on A.I.
item.description: Some iPhone owners will be eligible to receive $25 to $95 over claims that the tech giant oversold its artificial intelligence system, Apple Intelligence.
item.dc:creator: David McCabe and Kalley Huang
item.media:credit: Lucia Vazquez for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Apple store at Grand Central Station in Manhattan.

item.title: Coinbase Lays Off 14% of Employees as A.I. Changes Work
item.description: The largest U.S. crypto exchange said it was cutting jobs because of cryptocurrency market volatility and to “optimize” for the artificial intelligence era.
item.dc:creator: Natallie Rocha
item.media:credit: Gabby Jones for The New York Times
item.media:description: Coinbase, which went public in 2021, is cutting 14 percent of its work force.

item.title: Ford Says an Affordable Electric Pickup Truck is Still Coming Next Year
item.description: Ford Motor has written off $20 billion in electric vehicle investments but says it is forging ahead with an electric pickup that will sell for $30,000 next year.
item.dc:creator: Jack Ewing
item.category: Sports Utility Vehicles and Light Trucks
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Kate Medley for The New York Times
item.media:description: Recharging a Mustang Mach-E, Ford’s only electric vehicle right now.

item.title: Jeeves and Ask.com Shut Down After Almost 30 Years
item.description: The pioneering search engine shut down on May 1, after nearly 30 years in operation.
item.dc:creator: Ali Watkins
item.media:credit: Newscast/Universal Images Group, via Getty Images
item.media:description: The Ask.com search engine website, with the illustration of the character Jeeves.

item.title: Driving Electric in Costa Rica Is Surprisingly Doable
item.description: The charging network is spotty, but it’s a small country.
item.dc:creator: Jack Ewing
item.media:credit: Tony Cenicola/The New York Times
item.media:description: Costa Rica requires utilities to install fast-charging stations every 50 miles on national highways. But many chargers don’t work.

item.title: These Countries Embrace E.V.s to Avoid Oil Price Shocks
item.description: People in Costa Rica and other Latin American, Asian and African countries are increasingly buying electric vehicles to avoid spiking fuel prices.
item.dc:creator: Jack Ewing and Tony Cenicola
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
item.media:credit: Tony Cenicola/The New York Times

item.title: How Running Shoes Have Evolved, From Ancient Greece to a Record-Breaking Marathon Time
item.description: The race to near-weightlessness has been a driving force of innovation in running sneakers and helped lead to records shattering at the London Marathon.
item.dc:creator: Adeel Hassan
item.media:credit: Justin Tallis/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images

item.title: Pentagon Makes Deals With A.I. Companies to Expand Classified Work
item.description: The agreements with the technology companies come amid the Defense Department’s dispute with Anthropic.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes and Sheera Frenkel
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: Anthropic and the Pentagon have been locked in a debate over whether the company’s A.I. model could be used to pilot autonomous drones or work on domestic surveillance.

item.title: OpenAI’s Big Reset + A.I. in the Doctor’s Office + Talkie, a pre-1930s LLM
item.description: Will the rising tide of A.I. adoption lift all boats?
item.dc:creator: Kevin Roose, Casey Newton, Whitney Jones, Rachel Cohn, Vjeran Pavic, Daniel Ramirez, Dan Powell, Marion Lozano, Rowan Niemisto and Diane Wong
item.media:credit: Photo Illustration by The New York Times; Photo: Getty Images

item.title: Why So Many People Already Own Shares of Elon Musk’s SpaceX
item.description: Even before the rocket company holds a major initial public offering, many people own stock in it through so-called special purpose vehicles.
item.dc:creator: Erin Griffith and Ryan Mac
item.media:credit: Brandon Celi

item.title: Musk vs. Altman: What Is This Really About?
item.description: In a landmark trial between Elon Musk and OpenAI’s Sam Altman, the origins of OpenAI are being examined. The Times’s technology reporter Cade Metz explains what’s behind it all.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz, Melanie Bencosme, Nikolay Nikolov, Stephanie Swart, Rafaela Balster and Karl Mollohan

item.title: Apple Reports 17% Sales Jump, Powered by iPhones
item.description: John Ternus, who will take over in September, spoke publicly on an investor call for the first time since his new job was announced.
item.dc:creator: Kalley Huang
item.media:credit: Lucia Vazquez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Apple’s quarterly results on Thursday topped Wall Street’s expectations.

item.title: Elon Musk’s A.I. Claims of Danger Face Limits in OpenAI Trial
item.description: One of Elon Musk’s abiding fears is that A.I. could one day threaten humans. But the jurors deciding his suit against OpenAI probably won’t hear about it.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz and Mike Isaac
item.media:credit: Manuel Orbegozo/Reuters
item.media:description: Elon Musk returned to the witness stand in federal court for a third day on Thursday in what is expected to be a monthlong trial.

item.title: Elon Musk suffered a rare loss in his last jury trial.
item.dc:creator: Kate Conger and Natallie Rocha
item.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
item.media:description: Elon Musk arriving at the Phillip Burton Federal Building and U.S. Courthouse in San Francisco in March.

item.title: Musk says he ‘was a fool’ to provide OpenAI’s early funding.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz and Mike Isaac
item.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
item.media:description: Elon Musk’s testimony grew testy during cross-examination from OpenAI’s lawyer

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz and Mike Isaac

item.title: Palantir Is Making a French Chore Coat. Yes, That Palantir.
item.description: The data analysis company is selling a version of the jacket that it says will show its commitment to “re-industrializing America.”
item.dc:creator: Yola Mzizi
item.media:credit: Palantir
item.media:description: Palantir’s new chore coat comes in bright blue and black.

item.title: Struggling With Phone Addiction? Try These Remedies.
item.description: Counting minutes is a poor way to curb phone habits. Real antidotes, experts say, include mindful parenting, curated content and human connection.
item.dc:creator: Brian X. Chen
item.media:credit: Derek Abella
